Learning Python From a JavaScript Background (2): The Origin Story
Learning Python From a JavaScript Background
13th iThome Ironman Contest

Every programming language has its own little birth story. JavaScript's is famously chaotic (the whole "Java vs JavaScript is like dog vs hot dog" thing), so what about Python? Is its history just as fun? This post takes a quick look at where Python came from, why it's named after a comedy troupe, and what makes its syntax so clean.

JavaScript Core (64) - ES6: Arrow Functions - A Quick Intro
JavaScript Core
ES6: Arrow Functions

Time for a brand new chapter — arrow functions. This is a quick intro to the ES6 arrow function syntax: how it compares to a regular function expression, the shorthand tricks you can use to drop the return and the braces, and the one rule about when you can (and can't) skip the parameter parentheses.
/en/javascript/2021/08/01/js-core-63-let-const-exercises/
Let & Const Quiz: Can You Predict the Output Before Looking? (JS Core #63)
ES6: let & const

Time for some exercises on let and const. As always, the advice is the same: think each question through carefully before you peek at the answer. Ten quick questions covering the temporal dead zone, const with arrays and objects, hoisting, and which ESLint style to pick.
/en/javascript/2021/07/25/js-core-62-es6-eslint-let-const/
JavaScript Core (62) — ES6 Let & Const: Let ESLint Be Your Study Buddy
ES6: let and const

If you want to take your JavaScript up a level, I'd genuinely suggest giving ESLint a try. This post covers what ESLint is, why the Airbnb and Standard style guides matter, and a concrete reason why you shouldn't mix var with ES6's let and const.
/en/javascript/2021/07/18/javascript-core-61-let-hoisting-temporal-dead-zone/
Does let Get Hoisted? Meet the Temporal Dead Zone (JS Core #61)
JavaScript 核心篇
ES6 章節：Let 及 Const

Everyone knows var gets hoisted — but what about let? This post digs into whether let is hoisted too, why calling it early throws a ReferenceError instead of giving you undefined, and what the Temporal Dead Zone (TDZ) actually is. We'll also use a typeof trick to prove let really does get hoisted, just in a different way.
/en/javascript/2021/07/12/javascript-var-a-b-comma-operator/
What Does var a, b = (1, 2) Actually Output? (And Why)

A fun little JavaScript brain-teaser was making the rounds on my Facebook feed, and it happened to come up in our work Q&A channel too. So I'm writing it down. We break down var a, b = (1, 2) piece by piece — multiple variable declaration, the grouping operator, and the comma operator — to figure out exactly why a is undefined and b is 2.
/en/javascript/2021/07/10/javascript-core-60-let-const-practical-techniques/
JavaScript Core (60) - ES6: let & const - Practical Tricks for let and const

Last chapter covered the basics of let and const. This time we get into some real-world stuff — the classic interview loop-with-setTimeout gotcha, why let fixes it, the closure hiding inside it, and what const actually locks down when it points at an object (plus how Object.freeze and 'use strict' fit in).
